pi_items.source: File consistency check: path appears in task.json, setup_task.sh, and export_result.sh
File consistency check: path appears in task.json, setup_task.sh, and export_result.sh

Web search of H-rotor VAWT literature with NACA 0018 airfoils shows typical Cp range of 0.3-0.45 for various designs. Minimum value of 0.30 is at the lower end of documented ranges and is realistic for low-solidity designs or sub-optimal conditions.

Web search of H-rotor VAWT literature shows: (1) 'A maximum rotor power coefficient of 0.5 is obtained with the NACA 0018 airfoil' (Analysis of 2-Bladed NACA 0018 VAWT); (2) 'CP = 0.43 for H-Rotor using NACA0018-blades' (Rotor comparison study); (3) 'c_pmax = 0.51 at solidity of 0.3' (Aspect ratio study); (4) 'H-rotor VAWT achieved Cp = 0.7365 at TSR=3, solidity=0.25' (QBlade simulation, though this exceeds Betz limit). The typical documented maximum is 0.5-0.51, making 0.55 optimistic but not impossible under ideal conditions.

Web search of VAWT strut drag impact literature shows: (1) 5.1-6.6% reduction (modest design, Aihara 2022); (2) 9% reduction (optimized design); (3) 26% reduction (cylinder struts); (4) 47.5% reduction (worst case). A minimum of 1.0% is lower than any documented case in published research.

Web search shows documented strut drag losses up to 47.5% (worst case), with 26% being common for un-optimized designs. A maximum of 40.0% is within the realistic range of documented extreme cases and aligns with Aihara's 26% baseline and optimization potential to worse designs.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires VAWT baseline and strutted Cp simulations using QBlade with NACA 0018 airfoil, 3 blades, 2m radius, 4m height, 0.25m chord (solidity ~0.06). Typical H-rotor maximum Cp with NACA 0018 is 0.43-0.51 based on published literature. The metadata's min_cp_baseline of 0.30 is realistic. The max_cp_baseline of 0.55 exceeds typical published maxima of 0.5-0.51 but may be theoretically achievable. VAWT strut drag typically causes 5-26% power coefficient reduction in literature; the metadata minimum of 1.0% is lower than any published case, while 40.0% maximum is within extreme scenarios.
